# 🏋️**HOST C O R E TRAINING PROGRAM**

## ℹ️Introduction

---

### 👋**Welcome to the Host Training Program**

**![star2 emoji][image1]** **Introduction**

Welcome to the Host Training Program for Three Points Hospitality\! Over the next five days, you will be equipped with the skills and knowledge needed to excel in your role as a host at one of our fine establishments. We’re excited to have you on board and look forward to seeing you thrive\!

### 🎯**Purpose & Targets**

## **![white\_check\_mark emoji][image2] Purpose**

During your training period, use this program as a reference for each shift. You have **10 days** from your first training shift to complete and pass your test, which covers the material in this guide and in your Host Manual. You can take your test as early as day one of training if you've studied and feel comfortable with all the material. You'll be expected to demonstrate a thorough understanding of your duties and the ability to perform them efficiently alongside your co-workers. Successfully completing this test within the **10-day window** is required before you'll be placed on the regular host schedule.

---

## ![dart emoji][image3] **Targets**

**When you complete this training, you will be able to:**

* Greet and seat guests promptly and courteously.  
* Manage reservations and walk-in guests effectively.  
* Demonstrate proper phone etiquette and handle inquiries professionally.  
* Maintain the cleanliness and organization of the host area.  
* Handle special event bookings and guest requests with confidence.

---

## ![memo emoji][image4] **Example**

This training will teach you all about the role of a host at Three Points Hospitality, what you need to know, and how to perform your duties effectively. You'll learn about our team, the essential procedures in the Host Manual, and the importance of excellent guest service. You'll also gain expertise in managing reservations, seating guests, and maintaining the host area. All of this will equip you with the best possible start as the newest member of our host team.

### 💯**Required Testing**

### **Testing will include:**

* **![book emoji][image5]** Host Manual  
* ![weight\_lifting emoji][image6] Training Material

![fire emoji][image7] **Pro Tip:** After setting up the necessary resources and knowledge base, it is crucial to practice and refine the skills required for hosting. Hands-on experience in greeting guests, managing reservations, and understanding customer service are essential aspects of becoming a successful host. Participating in ongoing trainings and working in different dining settings can greatly contribute to honing these skills. Networking with other professionals in the field and exchanging ideas can also provide valuable insights and inspiration for improving guest experiences.

### ✴️**Day Training Schedules**

The C.O.R.E. Training Program offers a structured daily training schedule tailored to each position across all our locations, ensuring consistency and thoroughness in your learning experience. Throughout your training, you will engage in hands-on activities, role-specific tasks, and guided learning sessions, all aimed at preparing you to deliver exceptional service. Whether you are training as a server, bartender, host, or any other key position, this program will provide you with the tools necessary to succeed and grow in your role.

##

## **daily training schedule pertaining to your role.**

## 1️⃣Day 1: Orientation & Foundation Building

---

### 🎯**Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today is all about building a strong foundation. You'll learn about the company, your responsibilities as a host, and the layout of the venue. This includes how to greet guests, manage reservations, and understand operational procedures. You'll also begin working hands-on with to-go orders, phone etiquette, and table management.

## **Why It's Important**

As the first point of contact for guests, the host plays a critical role in shaping the guest experience. Mastering the fundamentals early sets the tone for professionalism, efficiency, and service excellence throughout your shifts.

## 

### **![mega emoji][image8] Welcome to Day 1 of Training\!**

### ✅**Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Arrival**

* **Check in with the manager on duty to submit your paperwork, clock in, and begin orientation.**  
  * *Punctuality reflects professionalism—be on time and ready to learn.*  
  * *Familiarize yourself with your trainer and the layout of the shift.*

## **Step 2: Welcome & Introduction**

* **Your trainer will walk you through the 5-day training program and expectations.**  
  * *Venue-specific adjustments may apply—ask questions if anything is unclear.*  
  * *Your experience level will help determine the pace of your training.*

## **Step 3: Company Policies & Procedures**

* **Review the Employee Handbook, focusing on the mission, values, and employment policies.**  
  * *Understand policies around attendance, uniforms, and shift expectations.*  
  * *Company values should guide your interactions with both guests and coworkers.*

## **Step 4: Uniforms, Personal Hygiene, Health & Safety Regulations, Serving Alcohol**

* **Understand professional appearance, hygiene expectations, and alcohol service training requirements.**  
  * *A clean, well-kept appearance builds trust with guests.*  
  * *Know the standards for health safety and legal alcohol protocols.*

## **Step 5: Payrolls and Scheduling**

* **Learn the payroll system, tip structures, Branch, and 7Shifts scheduling tools.**  
  * *Make sure you can access your schedule and understand how hours and tips are tracked.*  
  * *Stay on top of schedule changes and pay periods.*

## **Step 6: Customer Interaction and Experience**

* **Understand the importance of guest greetings, waitlist procedures, and phone etiquette.**  
  * *A friendly, genuine welcome sets the tone for each guest's visit.*  
  * *Practice basic phone scripts for answering calls and inquiries.*

## **Step 7: Host Operational Procedures**

* **Review responsibilities like reservations, to-go orders, and phone payment procedures.**  
  * *Learn to enter accurate guest info and communicate order details clearly.*  
  * *Handling calls with clarity and professionalism is key.*

## **Step 8: Event and Reservation Management**

* **Learn how to confirm, adjust, or cancel large party reservations.**  
  * *Understand event protocol for birthdays, graduations, and other large gatherings.*  
  * *Stay organized to prevent double bookings or missed reservations.*

## **Step 9: Administrative and General Information**

* **Review uniform standards, gift card processing, restaurant hours, and handling job applications.**  
  * *Know where to find resources, supplies, and extra menus.*  
  * *Be ready to assist with day-to-day logistics.*

## **Step 10: The Grand Tour**

* **Take a walkthrough of both FOH and BOH areas with your trainer.**  
  * *Note locations of bathrooms, host stands, manager office, storage, dish pit, and emergency exits.*  
  * *Understanding your environment helps you move quickly and efficiently.*

## **Step 11: Opening Procedures**

* **Complete the opening checklist with your trainer using the Jolt app.**  
  * *A successful shift starts with a well-prepared front desk.*  
  * *Learn to reset menus, restock materials, and prepare guest materials.*

## **Step 12: Table Management**

* **Learn table numbers and locations throughout the restaurant.**  
  * *Use diagrams or live walk-throughs to build your memory.*  
  * *Quick and accurate seating improves guest flow and service efficiency.*

## **Step 13: Guest Interaction**

* **Observe your trainer's guest greetings, both in-person and over the phone.**  
  * *Note tone, posture, eye contact, and professionalism.*  
  * *Begin practicing guest interactions under guidance.*

## **Step 14: To-Go Orders**

* **Learn how to input and process to-go orders, including remote payments.**  
  * *Practice confirming names, contact numbers, and repeating orders back.*  
  * *Accuracy and professionalism ensure repeat business.*

## **Step 15: Server Rotation & Seating Process**

* **Understand how to properly rotate tables and communicate with servers and kitchen.**  
  * *Balance server workload and maintain even guest distribution.*  
  * *Learn to announce covers to the kitchen and notify servers of new tables.*

## **Step 16: Reservations**

* **Use the Resy app on the iPad to make and manage reservations.**  
  * *Ensure full guest details are recorded and confirmed.*  
  * *Familiarity with the system reduces errors and double-bookings.*

## **Step 17: Notes Section**

* **Use the Notes feature in the iPad to track special requests and preferences.**  
  * *This allows personalized service and ensures team-wide communication.*  
  * *Review examples with your trainer for clarity.*

## **Step 18: Familiarization**

* **Take time to observe the flow of service and ask questions.**  
  * *Watch how other hosts interact, resolve issues, and manage seating.*  
  * *Use this time to reinforce knowledge and gain confidence.*

## **Step 19: End of Shift**

* **Wrap up your shift by reviewing the checklist and confirming with the manager.**  
  * *Double-check your responsibilities and complete final tasks.*  
  * *Reflect with your trainer on the day's progress and next steps.*

### ⭐**Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image9] Laying the Foundation for Front Door Excellence**

* **Venue Tour & Table Mapping:** Practice identifying tables and sections throughout the restaurant with your trainer.  
* **Phone Script Practice:** Role-play answering phones and taking reservation details.  
* **Live Guest Observations:** Watch your trainer handle greetings, issues, and reservations—then discuss what you observed.

---

## ![bulb emoji][image10] **Pro Tips**

* **Be the Smile Guests Remember:** A warm welcome can make someone's day.  
* **Organization Is Key:** Master the layout, iPad tools, and reservation flow to avoid mistakes.  
* **Ask Questions Early:** Don't wait—learning now prevents confusion later.

---

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image11] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "How do you properly seat a reservation versus a walk-in?"  
* **Scenario:** "A guest walks in with no reservation on a full night—how do you handle the situation professionally?"

---

## ![dart emoji][image12] **Performance Targets**

* **Venue Knowledge:** Identify table numbers, key guest areas, and back-of-house locations.  
* **Reservation & Seating Procedures:** Accurately take, confirm, and execute reservations.  
* **To-Go Order Accuracy:** Take and repeat back orders clearly and process payments.  
* **Guest Engagement:** Demonstrate polite and confident greetings and phone etiquette.

#### ![point\_right emoji][image13] **Do you feel confident that all targets were hit?**

#### **![point\_right emoji][image14] Review any targets you may have missed.**

### 🏁**Conclusion**

| By the end of Day 1, you should feel comfortable with the venue layout, confident in welcoming guests, and familiar with basic hosting responsibilities. You're laying the foundation for seamless service and professional growth. |
| :---: |

## ![pushpin emoji][image15] **Next Steps**

* **Complete the DAY 1 Training Session & Knowledge Check** **before arriving for Day 2\.**  
* **Review the Host Handbook and refresh your knowledge of table numbers and reservation process.**  
* **Practice using the iPad reservation system and table layout maps.**

# 

## 2️⃣Day 2: Hosting Fundamentals & Reservation Mastery

---

### 🎯**Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today is focused on mastering core hosting responsibilities—greeting guests, managing reservations, answering calls, and executing seating strategies. You'll also build confidence using the Resy app, handling phone interactions, and balancing walk-ins with scheduled reservations.

## **Why It's Important**

As a host, you're the first and last impression for guests. Your ability to manage the front door, organize reservations, and control the flow of the dining room directly impacts both guest satisfaction and overall efficiency. Precision and professionalism start here.

## **TIME TO SHOW THOSE SKILLS**

## 

### ✅**Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Independent Opening**

* **Execute opening procedures using the Jolt app with your trainer observing.**  
  * *Set up menus, iPads, and host stand materials accurately and on time.*  
  * *A clean, organized host stand sets the tone for service.*

## **Step 2: Reservation Expertise**

* **Learn to manage reservations using the Resy app efficiently.**  
  * *Review how to create, adjust, or cancel bookings.*  
  * *Understand how to block time slots, accommodate large parties, and prevent overbooking.*

## **Step 3: Answering Calls & Making Reservations**

* **Practice answering calls with professionalism and taking full reservation details.**  
  * *Gather guest name, phone number, email, and any special requests.*  
  * *Always speak clearly and confirm details back to the guest.*

## **Step 4: Strategic Seating**

* **Implement seating strategies to balance reservation flow and walk-ins.**  
  * *Review ideal table turnover pacing and section rotation.*  
  * *Avoid overloading any one section or server.*

## **Step 5: Order Management**

* **Handle to-go orders over the phone, confirming accuracy and contact details.**  
  * *Repeat the order back to the guest before ending the call.*  
  * *Mistake-free to-go orders improve guest trust and repeat business.*

## **Step 6: Seating Guests**

* **Seat guests at their reserved tables or accommodate walk-ins at available tables.**  
  * *Announce the guest to the server when seated and inform kitchen if needed.*  
  * *Your greeting and demeanor should make every guest feel like a VIP.*  
  * Review from the Host Manual: [Demonstrations, Points, & Tips](https://app.trainual.com/f9e05a9e-ccec-463e-beae-d1c5489f4c52/steps/19772651/consume)

## **Step 7: Team Coordination**

* **Practice server rotation, ensuring fair and balanced table distribution.**  
  * *Coordinate seating with server sections and availability.*  
  * *Effective rotation reduces errors, wait times, and missed service opportunities.*

## **Step 8: Technology Utilization**

* **Use the iPad efficiently to manage all hosting tasks.**  
  * *Leverage Notes, Floor View, Waitlist, and Reservation tools inside Resy.*  
  * *Keep the digital system up to date in real-time during your shift.*

## **Step 9: End of Shift**

* **Check in with the Manager on Duty (MOD) before clocking out.**  
  * *Ensure all host duties are complete and systems are updated.*  
  * *Ask for feedback on your progress and discuss any remaining questions.*

## **Step 10: Shift Wrap-Up & Checklist**

* **Review your progress and complete the end-of-day checklist.**  
  * *Take note of any areas needing more practice tomorrow.*  
  * *Reflect with your trainer on today's goals and achievements.*

### ⭐**Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image16] Strengthening Core Systems & Reservation Flow**

* **Reservation Drill:** Simulate high-volume booking scenarios and practice real-time edits and confirmations in Resy.  
* **Phone Etiquette Role-Play:** Practice call scripts with your trainer using real guest scenarios.  
* **Seating Flow Exercise:** Execute mock walkthroughs of seating guests from arrival to table handoff.

---

## ![bulb emoji][image17] **Pro Tips**

* **Details Matter:** Always repeat guest names and reservation times to prevent miscommunication.  
* **Stay One Step Ahead:** Anticipate party size surges and plan seating around peak times.  
* **Phone Professionalism:** Smile when you talk—it truly makes a difference in tone.

---

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image18] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "What steps do you take when adjusting an existing reservation in Resy?"  
* **Scenario:** "You receive a call for a reservation during an already booked time slot—how do you handle it?"

---

## ![dart emoji][image19] **Performance Targets**

* **Reservation Accuracy:** Confidently create and manage bookings in Resy.  
* **Guest Communication:** Answer calls clearly and collect complete reservation details.  
* **Seating Flow Management:** Execute seating strategy while balancing reservations and walk-ins.  
* **To-Go Order Clarity:** Confirm and input orders without error.

#### ![point\_right emoji][image20] **Do you feel confident that all targets were hit?**

#### **![point\_right emoji][image21] Review any targets you may have missed.**

### 🏁**Conclusion**

| By the end of Day 2, you should feel confident managing reservations, communicating over the phone, and seating guests with precision. You've built the technical and interpersonal foundation needed for smooth guest experiences and efficient front-of-house operations. |
| :---: |

### ![pushpin emoji][image22] **Next Steps**

* **Complete the DAY 2 Training Session & Knowledge Check before arriving for Day 3\.**  
* **Practice inputting reservations and seating charts in the Resy app.**  
* **Review the host handbook and prepare for high-stress guest scenarios.**

## 3️⃣Day 3: Guest Experience & High-Volume Hosting

---

### 🎯**Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today's training is all about refining your communication skills, navigating high-stress situations, handling guest concerns, and managing waitlists. You'll also begin applying all your hosting tools in real time, learning how to balance service flow during peak periods while maintaining a professional and welcoming attitude.

## **Why It's Important**

A great host isn't just welcoming—they're strategic, calm under pressure, and able to juggle guest needs in a fast-paced environment. Your ability to handle special requests, resolve concerns, and manage expectations directly impacts guest satisfaction and team coordination.

### ✅**Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Host Duties**

* **Clock in and begin working through your core responsibilities: calls, reservations, take-out orders, seating, and guest support.**  
  * *Consistency in these basics builds reliability and confidence.*  
  * *Use every task to sharpen your timing and organization.*

## **Step 2: Guidance**

* **Ask for support whenever you're unsure—trainers are there to help.**  
  * *Better to ask than to assume—this prevents costly guest errors.*  
  * *Learning collaboratively strengthens teamwork.*

## **Step 3: Handling Special Requests, Managing Waitlists, and Addressing Guest Concerns**

* **Work with your trainer through real scenarios, including dietary requests, unexpected parties, or guest dissatisfaction.**  
  * *Stay poised and positive in all guest interactions.*  
  * *Always communicate with servers or MOD when a concern escalates.*

## **Step 4: Problem Resolution**

* **Practice responding to guest complaints or service challenges calmly and quickly.**  
  * *De-escalation and clear solutions are your best tools.*  
  * *Empathy and follow-up help turn negative experiences around.*

## **Step 5: Common Rebuttals**

* **Review rebuttal scripts from the Host Handbook and practice with your trainer.**  
  * *Understand how to decline requests gracefully and redirect guests effectively.*  
  * *Consistency in messaging keeps service professional and fair.*

| Expo Training Session \#1 Objective: Introduction to Expo Training and Demonstrate Readiness for Independent Work |
| ----- |

*This segment focuses on mastering the expo role by efficiently coordinating between the kitchen and dining area. You must demonstrate readiness to work independently and grasp expo responsibilities. By the end, you should be confident in front-of-house duties and understand seamless restaurant operations.*

## **Your Tasks:**

* **Expo Station Setup:** Work with the current expo or manager on duty throughout this training session. Review items stocked for the dinner shift and ensure everything is set up with backup items ready to go.  
* **Expediting Food Orders:** Expedite food tickets with a trainer or manager. Run food to tables according to printed tickets.  
* **Understanding the Expo Role:** Learn the responsibilities of the expo position, including organizing and coordinating food orders, communicating with kitchen staff, and ensuring order accuracy.  
* **Station Cleanliness:** Maintain station cleanliness throughout the shift using sanitizer solution, sanitizer wipes, gloves, and rags.  
* **Expo Station Setup Recap:** Review the proper setup of the expo station, including organizing tickets, garnishes, condiments, and necessary utensils.  
* **Order Coordination:** Practice coordinating orders between the kitchen and serving staff. Ensure each dish is prepared to correct specifications and all components of an order are complete before sending it out.  
* **Quality Control:** Understand the importance of quality control. Check each plate for presentation, temperature, and accuracy before it leaves the kitchen.  
* **Timing and Efficiency:** Learn techniques for managing the timing of orders, especially during peak service times. Ensure hot foods remain hot and cold foods remain cold.  
* **Communication Skills:** Enhance your communication skills to efficiently relay information between kitchen staff and servers. Practice clear and concise communication to avoid misunderstandings.  
* **Handling Special Requests:** Learn how to handle special requests and modifications efficiently. Ensure all dietary restrictions and allergies are communicated clearly to the kitchen staff.

| Once you've completed the Cross-Training Expo Session, please find your assigned trainer for the remainder of your shift. |
| :---- |

## **Step 6: Shift Handover**

* **Learn how to properly brief management or the next host on the status of the floor.**  
  * *Share wait times, upcoming reservations, and any unresolved concerns.*  
  * *Strong handovers create continuity for guest service.*

## **Step 7: Late Service Management**

* **Maintain guest experience and attentiveness even during slower hours.**  
  * *Never drop standards at the end of the night—every guest deserves your best.*  
  * *Help the team wrap up efficiently while staying guest-focused.*

## **Step 8: Venue Security**

* **Conduct a final walkthrough to ensure guest areas are secure and clean.**  
  * *Check doors, bathrooms, and back areas—report anything unusual to MOD.*  
  * *Cleanliness and security protect the space for both staff and guests.*

## **Step 9: Closing Checklist**

* **Use the Jolt app to complete the host closing checklist thoroughly.**  
  * *Reset menus, tidy the host stand, and update the system for tomorrow.*  
  * *A complete close-down supports the next day's success.*

## **Step 10: End of Shift**

* **Check in with the MOD, complete your checklist, and review feedback before clocking out.**  
  * *Take note of any areas to revisit or improve.*  
  * *Always leave your shift with tasks fully completed.*

### ⭐**Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image23] Managing Pressure, Personalization & Problem Solving**

* **Rebuttal Role-Play:** Practice turning down table requests or managing guests without reservations using your handbook scripts.  
* **Waitlist & Walk-in Drill:** Simulate a full floor and practice placing walk-ins effectively without disrupting reservations.  
* **Problem-Solving Workshop:** Run through guest complaints with your trainer and craft the best responses.

---

## ![bulb emoji][image24] **Pro Tips**

* **Stay Cool, Stay Kind:** Guests remember how you made them feel—even in tough moments.  
* **Own the Experience:** If something goes wrong, take responsibility and offer a solution—guests respect accountability.  
* **Support the Team:** Helping servers and managers during peak times strengthens the whole flow.

---

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image25] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "What are the key steps to follow when a guest is unhappy with their seating arrangement?"  
* **Scenario:** "A walk-in party of six arrives during a fully booked hour—what do you do, and how do you communicate the wait time professionally?"

---

## ![dart emoji][image26] **Performance Targets**

* **Guest Conflict Resolution:** Respond to concerns with confidence, professionalism, and empathy.  
* **Waitlist Management:** Accurately place and monitor walk-ins using estimated wait times.  
* **Communication Proficiency:** Demonstrate calm, clear responses to guest objections.  
* **Closing Reliability:** Fully complete your shift handoff and closing checklist.

## 4️⃣Day 4: Team Integration & Cross-Training Collaboration

---

### 🎯**Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today is centered on collaborative service, mastering multitasking under pressure, and working with different departments to deliver seamless guest experiences. You'll strengthen your ability to communicate clearly, support your team, and refine your table management flow. This is also your second Cross-Training Expo Session, giving you a deeper understanding of the kitchen-to-dining-room connection.

## **Why It's Important**

A great host not only seats guests but acts as a bridge between departments. Strong communication, efficient multitasking, and awareness of everyone's role ensures a smooth shift and exceptional guest satisfaction. This day ensures you're part of the larger hospitality system—not just the front door.

# 

### ✅**Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Accelerated Setup**

* **Execute your opening routine efficiently and independently using the Jolt app.**  
  * *Make sure all tools, menus, and systems are ready before service starts.*  
  * *A seamless setup sets the pace for the rest of the shift.*

## **Step 2: Mastering Host Duties**

* **Work with your trainer through all key responsibilities: phones, reservations, orders, seating, and problem resolution.**  
  * *Consistency is key—repetition builds mastery.*  
  * *Ask for feedback to fine-tune performance and confidence.*

## **Step 3: Queue Optimization**

* **Learn how to manage guest flow and wait times during busy service periods.**  
  * *Use communication and timing to keep guests informed and happy.*  
  * *Avoid empty tables or excessive delays through careful pacing.*

## **Step 4: Multitask Mastery**

* **Practice balancing seating, phone calls, and take-out orders simultaneously.**  
  * *Set priorities based on time sensitivity and guest urgency.*  
  * *Refine your ability to switch tasks quickly without losing accuracy.*

## **Step 5: Cross-Functional Communication**

* **Practice clear, direct communication with servers, food runners, and kitchen staff.**  
  * *Announce seated guests, notify of large parties, and relay key guest info.*  
  * *Proactive communication prevents confusion and delays.*

| Cross-Training Session \#2 Objective: Complete your final Expo cross-training session to reinforce understanding of BOH/FOH operations. |
| ----- |

*This segment recaps key topics from earlier sessions, ensuring you understand the core responsibilities and skills for the expo role. It includes task reviews, Q\&A, and extra practice as needed.*

## **Your Tasks:**

* **Expo Station Setup Recap:** Review the process of setting up the expo station. Discuss any challenges encountered during the previous session and strategies to overcome them.  
* **Expediting Food Orders Recap:** Reflect on the experience of expediting food orders from the previous session. Discuss successes and areas for improvement.  
* **Understanding the Expo Role Recap:** Revisit the responsibilities of the expo position, with a focus on effective coordination and communication with the kitchen staff.  
* **Station Cleanliness Recap:** Review the importance of maintaining a clean and organized station. Discuss techniques and best practices for keeping the station sanitary.  
* **Order Coordination Recap:** Reflect on coordinating orders between the kitchen and serving staff. Identify any difficulties and discuss effective solutions.  
* **Quality Control Recap:** Review the importance of quality control. Discuss any instances where quality control was challenging and how to address them.  
* **Timing and Efficiency Recap:** Reflect on managing the timing of orders, especially during peak service times. Discuss any timing issues and how to improve overall efficiency.  
* **Communication Skills Recap:** Review the importance of clear and concise communication. Discuss any communication issues faced and how to improve them.  
* **Handling Special Requests Recap:** Reflect on handling special requests and modifications. Discuss any challenges encountered and how to improve accuracy.  
* **Final Review and Wrap-Up:** Summarize the key learnings from the session. Discuss any remaining questions or concerns with your trainer or the Manager on Duty (MOD).

#### 

| Once you've completed this final Cross-Training Expo Session, please find your assigned trainer for the remainder of your shift. |
| :---- |

## **Step 7: Shift Wrap-Up & Checklist**

* **Reflect on the day's work and complete your closing checklist.**  
  * *Check all systems, finalize notes in Resy, and restock materials.*  
  * *Discuss performance with your trainer and prep for final day of training.*

* **Multitasking Challenge:** Handle mock calls, to-go orders, and walk-ins at the same time to test efficiency.  
* **Expo Recap Debrief:** Walk through the kitchen workflow with your trainer and reflect on how it supports guest service.

---

* **Anticipate Needs:** Look ahead—who's walking in, who's about to leave, who needs a reset?  
* **Don't Rush, Flow:** You don't need to be fast, you need to be smooth. Pace beats panic.  
* **Keep the Team Informed:** Great hosts prevent breakdowns by keeping everyone in the loop.

---

* **Scenario:** "The dining room is full and waitlist is growing—how do you keep guests happy and staff organized?"

---

## ![dart emoji][image33] **Performance Targets**

* **Setup Efficiency:** Complete opening checklist independently and on time.  
* **Multitasking Confidence:** Balance hosting duties without compromising service quality.  
* **Team Communication:** Show confidence and clarity in your cross-functional communication.  
* **Expo Knowledge:** Demonstrate understanding of kitchen-to-dining coordination and its importance.

## 5️⃣Day 5: Final Evaluation & Solo Shift Readiness

---

### 🎯**Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today is about demonstrating everything you've learned. From handling reservations to greeting and seating guests, you'll put your skills into action during a full shift. You'll also complete your final cross-training session, review responsibilities, and participate in a comprehensive performance evaluation with your trainer.

## **Why It's Important**

This is your final day of guided training. It's your chance to show that you're ready to host independently, represent the brand, and support team flow during high-volume service. You'll solidify your confidence in hosting responsibilities and receive feedback for ongoing development.

### ✅**Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Autonomous Opening**

* **Execute all pre-service tasks independently using the Jolt app.**  
  * *Prepare the host stand, iPads, reservation tools, and menus without assistance.*  
  * *Check-in with the MOD to confirm readiness.*

## **Step 2: Advanced Guest Relations**

* **Manage guest needs, reservations, and seating while handling high-demand moments.**  
  * *Deliver warm greetings, clear communication, and solutions for special requests.*  
  * *Uphold service standards in every interaction.*

## **Step 3: Strategic Reservation Management**

* **Balance walk-ins, reservations, and large parties through strategic planning.**  
  * *Use Resy tools effectively for pacing and table optimization.*  
  * *Prevent overbooking or long waits with proactive adjustments.*

## **Step 4: Team Synergy**

* **Collaborate seamlessly with the kitchen, servers, food runners, and management.**  
  * *Announce parties clearly and support smooth table transitions.*  
  * *Relay important information between FOH and BOH efficiently.*

## **Step 5: Cross-Training Session \#2**

* **Complete your final cross-training session in Expo or a related department.**  
  * *Demonstrate your ability to support kitchen flow and guest service from behind the scenes.*  
  * *Connect this knowledge to how it impacts your role as a host.*

## **Step 6: Efficient Close-Down**

* **Lead the post-service tasks, completing the Jolt closing checklist.**  
  * *Ensure everything is reset, cleaned, and stocked for the next shift.*  
  * *Double-check that the system is updated and any notes are recorded.*

## **Step 7: Comprehensive Evaluation**

* **Participate in a full performance review with your trainer.**  
  * *Discuss your strengths, areas to improve, and next steps for development.*  
  * *Ask questions or address concerns before transitioning to solo shifts.*

## **Step 8: Shift Wrap-Up & Checklist**

* **Finalize all checklists and confirm completion with the MOD.**  
  * *Leave your area organized and updated.*  
  * *Ensure all tasks are checked off and questions are answered before clocking out.*

### ⭐**Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image37] Hosting with Confidence & Final Skill Execution**

* **Live Service Simulation:** Run the front door independently during a high-traffic period with trainer observation.  
* **Reservation Triage Challenge:** Manage last-minute reservations and walk-ins during a mock dinner rush.  
* **Final Reflection & Role-Play:** Respond to common guest scenarios and review previous hosting challenges with your trainer.

---

## ![bulb emoji][image38] **Pro Tips**

* **Stay Engaged Start to Finish:** From open to close, your energy impacts the entire guest experience.  
* **Plan Ahead:** Look at your reservation board like a chessboard—strategic moves prevent chaos.  
* **Lead With Confidence:** Guests respond to confident, clear, and courteous service—it starts with you.

---

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image39] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "How do you manage last-minute walk-ins when you're fully booked for the hour?"  
* **Scenario:** "A regular guest shows up late for a reservation and wants their favorite table—how do you balance policy with hospitality?"

---

## ![dart emoji][image40] **Performance Targets**

* **Full-Service Execution:** Demonstrate independence in hosting duties from open to close.  
* **Guest Interaction Excellence:** Handle complex requests and regular guest expectations with confidence.  
* **Reservation Flow Mastery:** Use Resy to manage reservations and walk-ins strategically.  
* **Leadership in Closure:** Execute closing duties accurately and support the team's end-of-shift flow.

## **Conclusion**

By the end of Day 5, you should feel ready and confident to work solo as a professional, efficient, and welcoming host. You've built the skills, learned the systems, and proven your readiness to uphold the standards of hospitality at the highest level.

### ✅**Host Final Examination**

#### **Final Examination Scheduling**

To complete your training, you must contact your Trainer and/or Manager on Duty (MOD) to schedule your final examinations. These are separate from the knowledge checks in Trainual and must be **completed within 10 days** of starting training.

Once you have finished all 5 days of training, **submit the form below** to notify management that you are ready to take your final examinations or need an extra day or two of studying.

# **(ENGAGED & REVISED) HOST C.O.R.E. TRAINING PROGRAM**

# **Day 1: Orientation & Foundation Building**

# **![dart emoji][image51] Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today is all about building a strong foundation. You'll learn about the company, your responsibilities as a host, and the layout of the venue. This includes how to greet guests, manage reservations, and understand operational procedures. You'll also begin working hands-on with to-go orders, phone etiquette, and table management.

## **Why It's Important**

As the first point of contact for guests, the host plays a critical role in shaping the guest experience. Mastering the fundamentals early sets the tone for professionalism, efficiency, and service excellence throughout your shifts.

# ![clipboard emoji][image52] **Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Arrival**

* **Check in with the manager on duty to submit your paperwork, clock in, and begin orientation.**  
  * *Punctuality reflects professionalism—be on time and ready to learn.*  
  * *Familiarize yourself with your trainer and the layout of the shift.*

## **Step 2: Welcome & Introduction**

* **Your trainer will walk you through the 5-day training program and expectations.**  
  * *Venue-specific adjustments may apply—ask questions if anything is unclear.*  
  * *Your experience level will help determine the pace of your training.*

## **Step 3: Company Policies & Procedures**

* **Review the Employee Handbook, focusing on the mission, values, and employment policies.**  
  * *Understand policies around attendance, uniforms, and shift expectations.*  
  * *Company values should guide your interactions with both guests and coworkers.*

## **Step 4: Uniforms, Personal Hygiene, Health & Safety Regulations, Serving Alcohol**

* **Understand professional appearance, hygiene expectations, and alcohol service training requirements.**  
  * *A clean, well-kept appearance builds trust with guests.*  
  * *Know the standards for health safety and legal alcohol protocols.*

## **Step 5: Payrolls and Scheduling**

* **Learn the payroll system, tip structures, Branch, and 7Shifts scheduling tools.**  
  * *Make sure you can access your schedule and understand how hours and tips are tracked.*  
  * *Stay on top of schedule changes and pay periods.*

## **Step 6: Customer Interaction and Experience**

* **Understand the importance of guest greetings, waitlist procedures, and phone etiquette.**  
  * *A friendly, genuine welcome sets the tone for each guest's visit.*  
  * *Practice basic phone scripts for answering calls and inquiries.*

## **Step 7: Host Operational Procedures**

* **Review responsibilities like reservations, to-go orders, and phone payment procedures.**  
  * *Learn to enter accurate guest info and communicate order details clearly.*  
  * *Handling calls with clarity and professionalism is key.*

## **Step 8: Event and Reservation Management**

* **Learn how to confirm, adjust, or cancel large party reservations.**  
  * *Understand event protocol for birthdays, graduations, and other large gatherings.*  
  * *Stay organized to prevent double bookings or missed reservations.*

## **Step 9: Administrative and General Information**

* **Review uniform standards, gift card processing, restaurant hours, and handling job applications.**  
  * *Know where to find resources, supplies, and extra menus.*  
  * *Be ready to assist with day-to-day logistics.*

## **Step 10: The Grand Tour**

* **Take a walkthrough of both FOH and BOH areas with your trainer.**  
  * *Note locations of bathrooms, host stands, manager office, storage, dish pit, and emergency exits.*  
  * *Understanding your environment helps you move quickly and efficiently.*

## **Step 11: Opening Procedures**

* **Complete the opening checklist with your trainer using the Jolt app.**  
  * *A successful shift starts with a well-prepared front desk.*  
  * *Learn to reset menus, restock materials, and prepare guest materials.*

## **Step 12: Table Management**

* **Learn table numbers and locations throughout the restaurant.**  
  * *Use diagrams or live walk-throughs to build your memory.*  
  * *Quick and accurate seating improves guest flow and service efficiency.*

## **Step 13: Guest Interaction**

* **Observe your trainer's guest greetings, both in-person and over the phone.**  
  * *Note tone, posture, eye contact, and professionalism.*  
  * *Begin practicing guest interactions under guidance.*

## **Step 14: To-Go Orders**

* **Learn how to input and process to-go orders, including remote payments.**  
  * *Practice confirming names, contact numbers, and repeating orders back.*  
  * *Accuracy and professionalism ensure repeat business.*

## **Step 15: Server Rotation & Seating Process**

* **Understand how to properly rotate tables and communicate with servers and kitchen.**  
  * *Balance server workload and maintain even guest distribution.*  
  * *Learn to announce covers to the kitchen and notify servers of new tables.*

## **Step 16: Reservations**

* **Use the Resy app on the iPad to make and manage reservations.**  
  * *Ensure full guest details are recorded and confirmed.*  
  * *Familiarity with the system reduces errors and double-bookings.*

## **Step 17: Notes Section**

* **Use the Notes feature in the iPad to track special requests and preferences.**  
  * *This allows personalized service and ensures team-wide communication.*  
  * *Review examples with your trainer for clarity.*

## **Step 18: Familiarization**

* **Take time to observe the flow of service and ask questions.**  
  * *Watch how other hosts interact, resolve issues, and manage seating.*  
  * *Use this time to reinforce knowledge and gain confidence.*

## **Step 19: End of Shift**

* **Wrap up your shift by reviewing the checklist and confirming with the manager.**  
  * *Double-check your responsibilities and complete final tasks.*  
  * *Reflect with your trainer on the day's progress and next steps.*

# *![pushpin emoji][image53]* **Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image54] Laying the Foundation for Front Door Excellence**

* **Venue Tour & Table Mapping:** Practice identifying tables and sections throughout the restaurant with your trainer.  
* **Phone Script Practice:** Role-play answering phones and taking reservation details.  
* **Live Guest Observations:** Watch your trainer handle greetings, issues, and reservations—then discuss what you observed.

## ![bulb emoji][image55] **Pro Tips**

* **Be the Smile Guests Remember:** A warm welcome can make someone's day.  
* **Organization Is Key:** Master the layout, iPad tools, and reservation flow to avoid mistakes.  
* **Ask Questions Early:** Don't wait—learning now prevents confusion later.

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image56] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "How do you properly seat a reservation versus a walk-in?"  
* **Scenario:** "A guest walks in with no reservation on a full night—how do you handle the situation professionally?"

## ![dart emoji][image57] **Performance Targets**

* **Venue Knowledge:** Identify table numbers, key guest areas, and back-of-house locations.  
* **Reservation & Seating Procedures:** Accurately take, confirm, and execute reservations.  
* **To-Go Order Accuracy:** Take and repeat back orders clearly and process payments.  
* **Guest Engagement:** Demonstrate polite and confident greetings and phone etiquette.

# **Conclusion**

By the end of Day 1, you should feel comfortable with the venue layout, confident in welcoming guests, and familiar with basic hosting responsibilities. You're laying the foundation for seamless service and professional growth.

## ![pushpin emoji][image60] **Next Steps**

* **Complete the DAY 1 Training Session & Knowledge Check** **before arriving for Day 2\.**

# **Day 2: Hosting Fundamentals & Reservation Mastery**

# **![dart emoji][image61] Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today is focused on mastering core hosting responsibilities—greeting guests, managing reservations, answering calls, and executing seating strategies. You'll also build confidence using the Resy app, handling phone interactions, and balancing walk-ins with scheduled reservations.

## **Why It's Important**

As a host, you're the first and last impression for guests. Your ability to manage the front door, organize reservations, and control the flow of the dining room directly impacts both guest satisfaction and overall efficiency. Precision and professionalism start here.

# ![clipboard emoji][image62] **Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Independent Opening**

* **Execute opening procedures using the Jolt app with your trainer observing.**  
  * *Set up menus, iPads, and host stand materials accurately and on time.*  
  * *A clean, organized host stand sets the tone for service.*

## **Step 2: Reservation Expertise**

* **Learn to manage reservations using the Resy app efficiently.**  
  * *Review how to create, adjust, or cancel bookings.*  
  * *Understand how to block time slots, accommodate large parties, and prevent overbooking.*

## **Step 3: Answering Calls & Making Reservations**

* **Practice answering calls with professionalism and taking full reservation details.**  
  * *Gather guest name, phone number, email, and any special requests.*  
  * *Always speak clearly and confirm details back to the guest.*

## **Step 4: Strategic Seating**

* **Implement seating strategies to balance reservation flow and walk-ins.**  
  * *Review ideal table turnover pacing and section rotation.*  
  * *Avoid overloading any one section or server.*

## **Step 5: Order Management**

* **Handle to-go orders over the phone, confirming accuracy and contact details.**  
  * *Repeat the order back to the guest before ending the call.*  
  * *Mistake-free to-go orders improve guest trust and repeat business.*

## **Step 6: Seating Guests**

* **Seat guests at reserved tables or walk-ins as availability allows.**  
  * *Announce the guest to the server when seated and inform kitchen if needed.*  
  * *Your greeting and demeanor should make every guest feel like a VIP.*

## **Step 7: Team Coordination**

* **Practice server rotation, ensuring fair and balanced table distribution.**  
  * *Coordinate seating with server sections and availability.*  
  * *Effective rotation reduces errors, wait times, and missed service opportunities.*

## **Step 8: Technology Utilization**

* **Use the iPad efficiently to manage all hosting tasks.**  
  * *Leverage Notes, Floor View, Waitlist, and Reservation tools inside Resy.*  
  * *Keep the digital system up to date in real-time during your shift.*

## **Step 9: End of Shift**

* **Check in with the Manager on Duty (MOD) before clocking out.**  
  * *Ensure all host duties are complete and systems are updated.*  
  * *Ask for feedback on your progress and discuss any remaining questions.*

## **Step 10: Shift Wrap-Up & Checklist**

* **Review your progress and complete the end-of-day checklist.**  
  * *Take note of any areas needing more practice tomorrow.*  
  * *Reflect with your trainer on today's goals and achievements.*

# *![pushpin emoji][image63]* **Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image64] Strengthening Core Systems & Reservation Flow**

* **Reservation Drill:** Simulate high-volume booking scenarios and practice real-time edits and confirmations in Resy.  
* **Phone Etiquette Role-Play:** Practice call scripts with your trainer using real guest scenarios.  
* **Seating Flow Exercise:** Execute mock walkthroughs of seating guests from arrival to table handoff.

## ![bulb emoji][image65] **Pro Tips**

* **Details Matter:** Always repeat guest names and reservation times to prevent miscommunication.  
* **Stay One Step Ahead:** Anticipate party size surges and plan seating around peak times.  
* **Phone Professionalism:** Smile when you talk—it truly makes a difference in tone.

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image66] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "What steps do you take when adjusting an existing reservation in Resy?"  
* **Scenario:** "You receive a call for a reservation during an already booked time slot—how do you handle it?"

## ![dart emoji][image67] **Performance Targets**

* **Reservation Accuracy:** Confidently create and manage bookings in Resy.  
* **Guest Communication:** Answer calls clearly and collect complete reservation details.  
* **Seating Flow Management:** Execute seating strategy while balancing reservations and walk-ins.  
* **To-Go Order Clarity:** Confirm and input orders without error.

By the end of Day 2, you should feel confident managing reservations, communicating over the phone, and seating guests with precision. You've built the technical and interpersonal foundation needed for smooth guest experiences and efficient front-of-house operations.

# **Day 3: Guest Experience & High-Volume Hosting**

# **![dart emoji][image71] Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today's training is all about refining your communication skills, navigating high-stress situations, handling guest concerns, and managing waitlists. You'll also begin applying all your hosting tools in real time, learning how to balance service flow during peak periods while maintaining a professional and welcoming attitude.

## **Why It's Important**

A great host isn't just welcoming—they're strategic, calm under pressure, and able to juggle guest needs in a fast-paced environment. Your ability to handle special requests, resolve concerns, and manage expectations directly impacts guest satisfaction and team coordination.

## ![clipboard emoji][image72] **Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Host Duties**

* **Clock in and begin working through your core responsibilities: calls, reservations, take-out orders, seating, and guest support.**  
  * *Consistency in these basics builds reliability and confidence.*  
  * *Use every task to sharpen your timing and organization.*

## **Step 2: Guidance**

* **Ask for support whenever you're unsure—trainers are there to help.**  
  * *Better to ask than to assume—this prevents costly guest errors.*  
  * *Learning collaboratively strengthens teamwork.*

## **Step 3: Handling Special Requests, Managing Waitlists, and Addressing Guest Concerns**

* **Work with your trainer through real scenarios, including dietary requests, unexpected parties, or guest dissatisfaction.**  
  * *Stay poised and positive in all guest interactions.*  
  * *Always communicate with servers or MOD when a concern escalates.*

## **Step 4: Problem Resolution**

* **Practice responding to guest complaints or service challenges calmly and quickly.**  
  * *De-escalation and clear solutions are your best tools.*  
  * *Empathy and follow-up help turn negative experiences around.*

## **Step 5: Common Rebuttals**

* **Review rebuttal scripts from the Host Handbook and practice with your trainer.**  
  * *Understand how to decline requests gracefully and redirect guests effectively.*  
  * *Consistency in messaging keeps service professional and fair.*

## **Step 6: Shift Handover**

* **Learn how to properly brief management or the next host on the status of the floor.**  
  * *Share wait times, upcoming reservations, and any unresolved concerns.*  
  * *Strong handovers create continuity for guest service.*

## **Step 7: Late Service Management**

* **Maintain guest experience and attentiveness even during slower hours.**  
  * *Never drop standards at the end of the night—every guest deserves your best.*  
  * *Help the team wrap up efficiently while staying guest-focused.*

## **Step 8: Venue Security**

* **Conduct a final walkthrough to ensure guest areas are secure and clean.**  
  * *Check doors, bathrooms, and back areas—report anything unusual to MOD.*  
  * *Cleanliness and security protect the space for both staff and guests.*

## **Step 9: Closing Checklist**

* **Use the Jolt app to complete the host closing checklist thoroughly.**  
  * *Reset menus, tidy the host stand, and update the system for tomorrow.*  
  * *A complete close-down supports the next day's success.*

## **Step 10: End of Shift**

* **Check in with the MOD, complete your checklist, and review feedback before clocking out.**  
  * *Take note of any areas to revisit or improve.*  
  * *Always leave your shift with tasks fully completed.*

# *![pushpin emoji][image73]* **Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image74] Managing Pressure, Personalization & Problem Solving**

* **Rebuttal Role-Play:** Practice turning down table requests or managing guests without reservations using your handbook scripts.  
* **Waitlist & Walk-in Drill:** Simulate a full floor and practice placing walk-ins effectively without disrupting reservations.  
* **Problem-Solving Workshop:** Run through guest complaints with your trainer and craft the best responses.

## ![bulb emoji][image75] **Pro Tips**

* **Stay Cool, Stay Kind:** Guests remember how you made them feel—even in tough moments.  
* **Own the Experience:** If something goes wrong, take responsibility and offer a solution—guests respect accountability.  
* **Support the Team:** Helping servers and managers during peak times strengthens the whole flow.

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image76] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "What are the key steps to follow when a guest is unhappy with their seating arrangement?"  
* **Scenario:** "A walk-in party of six arrives during a fully booked hour—what do you do, and how do you communicate the wait time professionally?"

## ![dart emoji][image77] **Performance Targets**

* **Guest Conflict Resolution:** Respond to concerns with confidence, professionalism, and empathy.  
* **Waitlist Management:** Accurately place and monitor walk-ins using estimated wait times.  
* **Communication Proficiency:** Demonstrate calm, clear responses to guest objections.  
* **Closing Reliability:** Fully complete your shift handoff and closing checklist.

# **Conclusion**

By the end of Day 3, you should feel comfortable managing busy service periods, resolving guest issues with poise, and maintaining professional standards under pressure. Today's focus on high-volume service and communication lays the groundwork for truly confident, independent hosting.

## ![pushpin emoji][image80] **Next Steps**

# **Day 4: Team Integration & Cross-Training Collaboration**

# **![dart emoji][image81] Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today is centered on collaborative service, mastering multitasking under pressure, and working with different departments to deliver seamless guest experiences. You'll strengthen your ability to communicate clearly, support your team, and refine your table management flow. This is also your second Cross-Training Expo Session, giving you a deeper understanding of the kitchen-to-dining-room connection.

## **Why It's Important**

A great host not only seats guests but acts as a bridge between departments. Strong communication, efficient multitasking, and awareness of everyone's role ensures a smooth shift and exceptional guest satisfaction. This day ensures you're part of the larger hospitality system—not just the front door.

# ![clipboard emoji][image82] **Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Accelerated Setup**

* **Execute your opening routine efficiently and independently using the Jolt app.**  
  * *Make sure all tools, menus, and systems are ready before service starts.*  
  * *A seamless setup sets the pace for the rest of the shift.*

## **Step 2: Mastering Host Duties**

* **Work with your trainer through all key responsibilities: phones, reservations, orders, seating, and problem resolution.**  
  * *Consistency is key—repetition builds mastery.*  
  * *Ask for feedback to fine-tune performance and confidence.*

## **Step 3: Queue Optimization**

* **Learn how to manage guest flow and wait times during busy service periods.**  
  * *Use communication and timing to keep guests informed and happy.*  
  * *Avoid empty tables or excessive delays through careful pacing.*

## **Step 4: Multitask Mastery**

* **Practice balancing seating, phone calls, and take-out orders simultaneously.**  
  * *Set priorities based on time sensitivity and guest urgency.*  
  * *Refine your ability to switch tasks quickly without losing accuracy.*

## **Step 5: Cross-Functional Communication**

* **Practice clear, direct communication with servers, food runners, and kitchen staff.**  
  * *Announce seated guests, notify of large parties, and relay key guest info.*  
  * *Proactive communication prevents confusion and delays.*

## **Step 6: Cross-Training Session \#2**

* **Complete your final Expo cross-training session to reinforce understanding of BOH/FOH operations.**  
  * *Recap key expo responsibilities: order coordination, plating accuracy, cleanliness, and timing.*  
  * *Discuss any challenges and review how this role supports host operations.*

## **Step 7: Shift Wrap-Up & Checklist**

* **Reflect on the day's work and complete your closing checklist.**  
  * *Check all systems, finalize notes in Resy, and restock materials.*  
  * *Discuss performance with your trainer and prep for final day of training.*

# *![pushpin emoji][image83]* **Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image84] Syncing With the Team & Leading the Flow**

* **Wait Time Simulation:** Adjust and manage waitlists and walk-ins during a mock busy shift.  
* **Multitasking Challenge:** Handle mock calls, to-go orders, and walk-ins at the same time to test efficiency.  
* **Expo Recap Debrief:** Walk through the kitchen workflow with your trainer and reflect on how it supports guest service.

## ![bulb emoji][image85] **Pro Tips**

* **Anticipate Needs:** Look ahead—who's walking in, who's about to leave, who needs a reset?  
* **Don't Rush, Flow:** You don't need to be fast, you need to be smooth. Pace beats panic.  
* **Keep the Team Informed:** Great hosts prevent breakdowns by keeping everyone in the loop.

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image86] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "How do you balance answering phones while managing the floor and take-out orders?"  
* **Scenario:** "The dining room is full and waitlist is growing—how do you keep guests happy and staff organized?"

## ![dart emoji][image87] **Performance Targets**

* **Setup Efficiency:** Complete opening checklist independently and on time.  
* **Multitasking Confidence:** Balance hosting duties without compromising service quality.  
* **Team Communication:** Show confidence and clarity in your cross-functional communication.  
* **Expo Knowledge:** Demonstrate understanding of kitchen-to-dining coordination and its importance.

#### ![point\_right emoji][image88] **Do you feel confident that all targets were hit?**

#### **![point\_right emoji][image89] Review any targets you may have missed.**

# **Conclusion**

By the end of Day 4, you should feel fully integrated into the team, comfortable with multitasking during high-volume shifts, and confident in your communication with both guests and coworkers. You've reinforced your hosting foundation with strong teamwork and operational awareness.

## ![pushpin emoji][image90] **Next Steps**

* **Complete the DAY 4 Training Session & Knowledge Check** **before arriving for Day 5\.**  
* **Review Expo responsibilities and revisit table flow scenarios.**  
* **Ask final questions to prepare for independent hosting and your performance review tomorrow.**

# **Day 5: Final Evaluation & Solo Shift Readiness**

# **![dart emoji][image91] Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today is about demonstrating everything you've learned. From handling reservations to greeting and seating guests, you'll put your skills into action during a full shift. You'll also complete your final cross-training session, review responsibilities, and participate in a comprehensive performance evaluation with your trainer.

## **Why It's Important**

This is your final day of guided training. It's your chance to show that you're ready to host independently, represent the brand, and support team flow during high-volume service. You'll solidify your confidence in hosting responsibilities and receive feedback for ongoing development.

# ![clipboard emoji][image92] **Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Autonomous Opening**

* **Execute all pre-service tasks independently using the Jolt app.**  
  * *Prepare the host stand, iPads, reservation tools, and menus without assistance.*  
  * *Check-in with the MOD to confirm readiness.*

## **Step 2: Advanced Guest Relations**

* **Manage guest needs, reservations, and seating while handling high-demand moments.**  
  * *Deliver warm greetings, clear communication, and solutions for special requests.*  
  * *Uphold service standards in every interaction.*

## **Step 3: Strategic Reservation Management**

* **Balance walk-ins, reservations, and large parties through strategic planning.**  
  * *Use Resy tools effectively for pacing and table optimization.*  
  * *Prevent overbooking or long waits with proactive adjustments.*

## **Step 4: Team Synergy**

* **Collaborate seamlessly with the kitchen, servers, food runners, and management.**  
  * *Announce parties clearly and support smooth table transitions.*  
  * *Relay important information between FOH and BOH efficiently.*

## **Step 5: Cross-Training Session \#2**

* **Complete your final cross-training session in Expo or a related department.**  
  * *Demonstrate your ability to support kitchen flow and guest service from behind the scenes.*  
  * *Connect this knowledge to how it impacts your role as a host.*

## **Step 6: Efficient Close-Down**

* **Lead the post-service tasks, completing the Jolt closing checklist.**  
  * *Ensure everything is reset, cleaned, and stocked for the next shift.*  
  * *Double-check that the system is updated and any notes are recorded.*

## **Step 7: Comprehensive Evaluation**

* **Participate in a full performance review with your trainer.**  
  * *Discuss your strengths, areas to improve, and next steps for development.*  
  * *Ask questions or address concerns before transitioning to solo shifts.*

## **Step 8: Shift Wrap-Up & Checklist**

* **Finalize all checklists and confirm completion with the MOD.**  
  * *Leave your area organized and updated.*  
  * *Ensure all tasks are checked off and questions are answered before clocking out.*

# *![pushpin emoji][image93]* **Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image94] Hosting with Confidence & Final Skill Execution**

* **Live Service Simulation:** Run the front door independently during a high-traffic period with trainer observation.  
* **Reservation Triage Challenge:** Manage last-minute reservations and walk-ins during a mock dinner rush.  
* **Final Reflection & Role-Play:** Respond to common guest scenarios and review previous hosting challenges with your trainer.

## ![bulb emoji][image95] **Pro Tips**

* **Stay Engaged Start to Finish:** From open to close, your energy impacts the entire guest experience.  
* **Plan Ahead:** Look at your reservation board like a chessboard—strategic moves prevent chaos.  
* **Lead With Confidence:** Guests respond to confident, clear, and courteous service—it starts with you.

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image96] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "How do you manage last-minute walk-ins when you're fully booked for the hour?"  
* **Scenario:** "A regular guest shows up late for a reservation and wants their favorite table—how do you balance policy with hospitality?"

## ![dart emoji][image97] **Performance Targets**

* **Full-Service Execution:** Demonstrate independence in hosting duties from open to close.  
* **Guest Interaction Excellence:** Handle complex requests and regular guest expectations with confidence.  
* **Reservation Flow Mastery:** Use Resy to manage reservations and walk-ins strategically.  
* **Leadership in Closure:** Execute closing duties accurately and support the team's end-of-shift flow.

#### ![point\_right emoji][image98] **Do you feel confident that all targets were hit?**

#### **![point\_right emoji][image99] Review any targets you may have missed.**

## **Conclusion**

By the end of Day 5, you should feel ready and confident to work solo as a professional, efficient, and welcoming host. You've built the skills, learned the systems, and proven your readiness to uphold the standards of hospitality at the highest level.

## ![pushpin emoji][image100] **Next Steps**

* **Complete the DAY 5 Training Session & Knowledge Check before your first solo shift.**  
* **Review the Host Handbook and reservation procedures to stay sharp.**  
* **Prepare for any final evaluations or mock shifts with your manager.**  
* **Step into your role with pride—you're ready to lead the guest experience\!**

## 1️⃣OLD: Day 1 Training – Orientation Phase

---

### 💡**Objective & Overview**

### **![mega emoji][image101] Welcome to Day 1 of Training\!**

As you begin your journey as a host, this training checklist will be your guide for the day. It’s designed to help you understand the fundamental responsibilities of hosting, familiarize yourself with the restaurant layout, and start building the skills you’ll need to provide excellent service to our guests. Each item on the checklist is a crucial step in your development, ensuring that by the end of the day, you have a solid foundation in place. Take your time with each task, ask questions when needed, and use this opportunity to absorb as much knowledge as possible. Let’s make the most of today’s training\!

---

![pushpin emoji][image102] **Objective**: Orientation to Company, Introduction to Hosting, Operational Knowledge & Guest Service Fundamentals

![bulb emoji][image103] **Overview:** Welcome to your first day\! Today, you'll get an overview of our restaurant's unique atmosphere and what makes us special. We'll give you a comprehensive tour of the venue, including key areas such as entrances, exits, restrooms, and staff areas. Understanding our layout is crucial for managing reservations and seating guests efficiently. Remember, you are the first impression of our restaurant, so a welcoming demeanor is essential.

### 😊**Arrival**

Arrive promptly and find the manager on duty to give your paperwork, clock-in, and get acclimated

### 👋**Welcome & Introduction**

After your manager introduces you to your trainer, they will present an overview of our foundational 5-day training program, including on-site and off-site expectations. Additional location-specific training will be tailored based on venue requirements. Any relatable experience you have will be factored into the pace of your training process.

***{Reading} Employee Handbook Overview***

*This will be brief as you should have already read through, completed, and taken the test at the end.*

### 📜**Company Policies & Procedures**

Review Employee Handbook starting with the company’s practice (Mission, Vision, Core), employment policies, attendance, required uniform, and training outline

* Pay close attention to the company’s core values; they will guide your daily interactions and decisions.

***{Reading} Employee Handbook Overview***

*This will be brief as you should have already read through, completed, and taken the test at the end.*

### 📑**Uniforms, Personal Hygiene, Health & Safety Regulations, Serving Alcohol**

Understand the importance of personal appearance and hygiene practices, alcohol agreement & training

* Maintaining a professional appearance and following hygiene practices are crucial for creating a positive first impression.

***{Reading} Employee Handbook Overview***

*This will be brief as you should have already read through, completed, and taken the test at the end.*

### 💵**Payrolls and Scheduling**

The trainer will go over with you the payroll system, tip reduction %, CC & cash tips, Branch, & 7Shifts scheduling.

* Familiarize yourself with the scheduling system early on to avoid any confusion or missed shifts.

### 🤝**Customer Interaction and Experience**

Overview of the importance of a host, procedures for seating guests, waiting list, and phone scripts

* As the first point of contact, your demeanor sets the tone for the guest’s entire dining experience.

### 🛠️**Host Operational Procedures**

Review all responsibilities, overview of making reservations, To-Go Orders, and paying over the phone

* Understanding the reservation system and to-go orders ensures a smooth operation and satisfied customers.

### 🎉**Event and Reservation Management**

Overview of large parties and events, graduation & birthday reservations, and confirming, editing, or canceling reservations

* Efficient management of reservations, especially for large parties, is key to maintaining a well-organized seating plan.

### 📝**Administrative and General Information**

Overview of uniform standards, restaurant information, accepting and handling applications, gift cards, and hours of operation

* Keep track of all administrative details as they help you maintain smooth day-to-day operations.

### ✈️**The Grand Tour**

#### **Your trainer will give you a tour of the venue:**

**Front of House (FOH)** – Location of the bar area, guest bathrooms, inside/outside dining area, and private dining {location based}

**Back-of-House (BOH)** – Drink station, lockers, employee bathrooms, manager office (supplies, backup menus, keys for bathroom toiletries), kitchen/dish pit, dry storage for toilet paper and paper towels, and back entryways (location-based)

* Knowing the layout of both FOH and BOH allows you to assist guests and colleagues more efficiently.

### 📋**Opening Procedures**

Go through the opening checklist using the Jolt app with your trainer

* A thorough opening ensures that everything is in place for a smooth shift.

### 🪑**Table Management**

Go through and learn table numbers and locations

* Learning table numbers quickly helps you seat guests efficiently and manage the dining area better.

### 🙋**Guest Interaction**

Observe and analyze professional techniques for guest greetings and phone interactions

* Observing seasoned hosts will give you practical insights into how to handle various guest scenarios.

### 🥡**To-Go Orders**

With your trainer, learn how to place to-go orders and handling remote payments over the phone

* Double-checking orders over the phone prevents mistakes and ensures guest satisfaction.

### 🔄**Server Rotation & Seating Process**

Understand the process of server rotation. Listen, learn, and practice as your trainer goes over the process for seating guests: From welcoming them, taking them to their table, advising the server of their table, and informing the kitchen on how many active covers sat

* Properly managing server rotations prevents any one server from becoming overwhelmed, ensuring balanced service.

### 📅**Reservations**

Learn how to make reservations using the Resy app on the iPad

* Accurately managing reservations is crucial for avoiding double bookings and ensuring guest satisfaction.

### 📝**Notes Section**

Familiarize yourself with the Notes section of the iPad

* Use the Notes section to keep track of special guest requests or important details.

### 👀**Familiarization**

Take notice of how things are operating and ask questions

* Ask questions whenever you're unsure; it’s better to clarify now than make mistakes later.

---

### 💡**Objective & Overview**

### **![mega emoji][image111] Welcome to Day 2 of Training\!**

Building on what you learned on your first day, today’s training will focus on the core responsibilities of hosting, including greeting guests, managing reservations, and arranging seating. This checklist will guide you through these essential tasks, helping you become more confident and competent in your role as a host. Each item on the checklist is designed to enhance your understanding and practical abilities, ensuring that you’re ready to provide excellent service to our guests. Engage fully with each task, and don’t hesitate to seek clarification if needed. Let’s continue building on your foundation and make the most of today’s training\!

---

![pushpin emoji][image112] **Objective**: Hosting Fundamentals, Greeting Guests, Managing Reservations, & Seating Arrangements

![bulb emoji][image113] **Overview**: Today, we'll dive into the core responsibilities that make our hosting team exceptional. You'll practice greeting guests with a smile, managing our reservation system, and arranging seating to optimize the dining experience. By the end of the day, you'll be familiar with handling phone inquiries and using the Resy App for reservations. Effective communication and a friendly attitude are key components of a successful host.

### 🫵**Independent Opening**

Execute opening procedures using the Jolt app with your trainer shadowing you to observe your work and provide guidance

* Being able to handle the opening procedures independently is a strong indicator of your readiness.

### 📅**Reservation Expertise**

Master the Resy app for efficient and accurate reservation management

* Mastering the reservation system allows you to manage guest flow effectively, even during peak times.

### 📲**Answering Calls & Making Reservations**

Always be police and professional, while gathering all necessary details including telephone numbers, first and last names, and associated email addresses for reservation requests

* Clear, polite communication over the phone reflects well on the restaurant and ensures accurate reservations.

### 🧠**Strategic Seating**

Implementation of effective seating strategies to maximize restaurant capacity

* Implement seating strategies that maximize table turnover without sacrificing guest comfort.

### 📦**Order Management**

Handle to-go orders with precision, collecting guest’s name and telephone number, and repeating the order back to them before ending the call. Following these procedures will ensure accuracy and customer satisfaction

* Repeating back orders during phone calls minimizes mistakes and shows attentiveness.

### 🚻**Seating Guests**

Seat guests at their respective reserved table or walk-ins at available tables

* A warm and welcoming demeanor when seating guests enhances their overall dining experience.

### 🤝**Team Coordination**

Execute the server rotation system to maintain fair table distribution. Effective server rotation ensures all guests receive timely service, preventing any server from becoming overwhelmed.

* Effective server rotation ensures all guests receive timely service, preventing any server from becoming overwhelmed.

### 💻**Technology Utilization**

Leverage the iPad system to enhance operational efficiency

* Leveraging technology, like the iPad system, helps streamline operations and keep everything organized.

### 🕘**End of Shift**

At the end of your shift, find the Manager on Duty (MOD) and see if there is any additional work needed. If all duties are met, an evaluation on your current progress will be conducted before you clock out

---

### 💡**Objective & Overview**

### **![mega emoji][image121] Welcome to Day 3 of Training\!**

Today’s training is all about refining your interaction skills, handling special requests, and managing the challenges of a high-volume environment. This checklist will guide you through tasks that require strong communication skills, quick thinking, and the ability to maintain a calm and professional demeanor under pressure. Each item is an opportunity to demonstrate your ability to handle the demands of hosting during a busy shift. Focus on providing exceptional service and building strong relationships with guests. Let’s aim for excellence today and make sure you’re prepared for any scenario\!

---

![pushpin emoji][image122] **Objective**: Customer Service & Communication, Upselling, High Volume & High Stress

![bulb emoji][image123] **Overview:** Building on your foundation, today is all about refining your interaction skills. You'll learn how to handle special requests, manage waitlists, and address any guest concerns with poise and professionalism. Your trainer will go over various scenarios and rebuttals to ensure you're prepared for anything. Remember, our goal is to make every guest feel valued and cared for from the moment they walk in.

### 📋**Host Duties**

Arrive promptly to your shift, clock-in, and begin working with your trainer in all host related tasks and responsibilities including: Answering phone calls, reservations, take-out orders, seating guests, and handling any issues pertaining to your role

* Consistent performance of basic duties sets the stage for a smooth and efficient shift.

### 📢**Guidance**

Ask your trainer for any help or guidance if you feel you need it. It is better to do it right with help than do it wrong on your own

* Never hesitate to ask for help—it's better to learn the correct method than to guess and make mistakes.

### 🙋**Special Requests, Waitlists, & Addressing Guest Concerns**

Work with your trainer on handling multiple scenarios (i.g. dietary restrictions, guests arriving without reservations during peak hours, unhappy seating arrangements, significant wait times with large parties and seat requests)

* Stay calm and composed when handling special requests or complaints; your professionalism will impress guests and colleagues.

### 🔧**Problem Resolution**

Effectively address and resolve guest concerns as they arise

* Quick, effective problem-solving can turn a negative situation into a positive one, enhancing the guest experience.

### 📃**Common Rebuttals**

Review with your trainer, and as mentioned in the Host Manual, common rebuttals and effective ways to deal with them.

* Familiarize yourself with common rebuttals to be prepared for any guest objections or concerns:

### ↩️**Shift Handover**

Learning how to provide a comprehensive briefing to management on evening operations

* A thorough shift handover ensures that the incoming staff is fully briefed and ready to maintain service standards.

### 🌙**Late Service Management**

Maintain high service standards during slower evening hours

* Maintaining high standards during slower hours is just as important as during peak times; consistency is key.

### 🔏**Venue Security**

Conduct a thorough final walk-through, ensuring the restaurant is secured

* A final walk-through to ensure the venue is secure is essential for the safety of the staff and property.

### 📋**Closing Checklist**

Perform the mid/closing checklist using the Jolt app. If closing, this can begin around 30 minutes before the venue stops seating

* Completing the checklist thoroughly ensures everything is prepared and ready for the next shift, avoiding unnecessary delays.

### 🕘**End of Shift**

Find your manager and let them know you are done. They will advise if you have any further tasks to do or if you can clock out. Ensure you have completed the Shift Wrap-Up & Checklist with your trainer before doing so.

* Always confirm with your manager that all duties are completed before clocking out.
